json.content:
n8n Cloud vs Self-Host

n8n infrastructure isn't just about cost - it's about control, compliance, and where your data lives.

Here's the technical architecture comparison: 

𝗻𝟴𝗻 𝗖𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱
→ Instant setup in minutes
→ Auto-scaling for workflows
→ Hosted in Frankfurt (GDPR compliant)
→ Managed updates, built-in support

𝗦𝗲𝗹𝗳-𝗛𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗱
→ Community edition: FREE
→ Data never leaves your servers
→ Unlimited executions and active workflows
→ Full infrastructure control

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆:

→ For Zapier's Professional plan starts at $19/month for 750 tasks.
If you need 100K tasks, that scales to hundreds per month.

→ For n8n self-hosted? 
$0 to n8n. Unlimited.

𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗼 𝗦𝗲𝗹𝗳-𝗛𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗻8𝗻 𝗟𝗼𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘆:

1. Install Docker
2. Run this single command:
docker run -it --rm --name n8n -p 5678:5678 -v n8n_data:/home/node/.n8n docker.n8n.io/n8nio/n8n

3. Access at http://localhost:5678

That's it. Your workflows save to ./n8n_data folder.

When Each Wins:
→ Cloud: Quick start, no DevOps needed
→ Self-host: Regulated industries (finance, healthcare), high-volume workflows

n8n's Fair Code license (100k+ GitHub stars) means you can start cloud, migrate anytime. No lock-in.

Unlike cloud-only platforms or complex self-host tools, n8n offers both paths with 400+ integrations.

Your infrastructure choice: Control vs Convenience.

Cold Email Funnel For You To Hit Your Q3 goals!

Let’s Speak the Truth:

Your cold email will never scale without a working funnel.

- Not more tools.
- Not better copy.
 - Not AI agents.

Your cold email funnel = your growth system.

It’s how you convert cold leads into clients.

The 4-Stage Funnel I Use (and Build for Clients)

1. Infrastructure

You can use Saleshandy — it centralizes everything under one roof:
 • Email-sending automation
 • Lead finder database
 • Email infrastructure setup
 • Unified inbox
 • Analytics, and more

It also offers white-label portals, unlimited client workspaces, and centralized campaign management.

2. List Building

Cold Outbound That Doesn’t Feel Cold

Want outbound that actually builds trust instead of breaking it?

Here’s how to do it better:

LinkedIn: The Two-List System

A-List (Dream Clients):
 → Turn their problems into content
 → Collaborate, don’t pitch

B-List (Wider Audience):
 → Run group webinars
 → Nurture through community

Cold doesn't have to mean distant. 

Lead with value, and the right people lean in.

3. Copy

Cold Emails That Don’t Feel Like Spam

A-List Email Template:

Hi [Name],
I’ve been following [specific thing about their company], and I think your insights on [topic] would be incredible to share. I’m organizing a webinar on “[Industry Challenge/Trend]” and would love to have you as a co-host.
It’s a great opportunity to showcase your expertise while connecting with [specific audience].
Can we hop on a quick call to discuss?
Cheers
Your Name

B-List Email Template:

Hey [Name],
I’m hosting a free webinar for [Your Niche] on “[Problem They Struggle With],” and I think it’d be awesome to co-host with you. Your experience in [specific area] would add so much value for the audience.
Let me know if you’re up for it — we can brainstorm over a quick chat.
Best,
Your Name

4. Community:

Create multiple Reddit accounts (using proxies):

 • Use karma-farming subreddits or post clickbait-style content to build karma
 • Then shift to posting casual, informative content
 • Attach your newsletter as a resource

Warm email those leads

Random tools and AI agents won’t get you results.

1) Use Explee to build your lead list in under 60 seconds

Company search: 
→ “B2B SaaS companies in Singapore” 
People search:  → “Founders”

You get:
- Leads + emails - Matches your ICP - Already verified and ready to use - You only pay if ZeroBounce confirms the email 
Step 2: Webinar Strategy

If you sell high-ticket, you probably need 2-3 calls to close one deal.

This funnel saves time:

1) Send a cold email, but make it feel like an exclusive invite 2) Host a webinar → show everything live once 3) Add leads to a Slack/WhatsApp group 4) They warm up inside the group and ask for a pitch themselves

Step 3: Community Strategy

Turn your webinar + other platforms into a funnel.

1) Pull in your ICP from Reddit, Slack groups, and your own webinar 
2) Host your own Slack, Discord, or WhatsApp community 
3) Post daily—polls, shoutouts, short content  4) Pitch them through the value you share

How to Start: Week-by-Week Timeline

Week 1: Dream Client List 
1) Write your top 10 dream clients.
 2) Figure out their problems → Use that info to build your list using Explee.

Week 2: Setup Systems

- LinkedIn: Set up a basic outreach flow - Email: Build list using Explee → run with Smartlead - Content: Plan 3 posts each for LinkedIn, Instagram, and Reddit  
Weeks 3–4: Start Outreach

1) Reach out to A-list and B-list leads with co-host invites or collab angles 2) Post regularly and reply to comments + DMs

Month 2: Build Your Community

1) Launch a Slack or Discord group 2) Invite your leads and drop helpful content 3) Host your first webinar or AMA inside

So I thought I'd share my ten rules for my AI SDR that books me on average 50-100 calls per month:

1. For interested prospects, you'll guide them to {{goal}} through this link ({{goalLink}}). 

2. For those not interested, you'll encourage them to {{fallback}} through this link {{fallbackLink}}.

3. Keep messages under 30 words 

4. Avoid starting your sentence with a verb, as this can make your writing feel abrupt or too commanding 

5. Write as if speaking orally 

6. Use pragmatic, straight-to-point language 

7. Maintain moderate number of questions throughout conversation 

8. Show genuine interest without being scripted 

9. Never appear robotic or formal 

10. Don't send any link too quick but don't be too slow if the prospect is interested

json.content:
There’s ONLY 3 actions you need to perform to get leads on LinkedIn:
→ Posting
→ DMing
→ Engaging

But you probably know that already, so let me get a bit more specific…

1. Problem-Focused Content
- Target ONE specific pain point per post
- Show you understand their struggles
- Present clear, actionable solutions
- Skip the motivation, focus on transformation

2. High-Value DM Strategy
- No templates or scripts
- Mention a competitor
- Focus on their goals first
- One-liner pitch with clear CTA

3. Strategic Engagement
- Add PERSONAL insights that expand on the original post
- Share real experiences
- Focus on decision-makers' content
- Skip generic comments (there’s no point)

This is everything you need for the perfect All-bound funnel.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 6 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

json.content:
𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐦 𝐬𝐚𝐰 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐡𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐥𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐥𝐝 𝐝𝐢𝐝.

All of us started our careers like this —
 🧳 1 room
 🎒 2 bags
 💼 New job
 📍 New city

No fancy furniture. Just a suitcase, a mattress on the floor, and a head full of dreams.
We learned how to budget ₹5000 across rent, food, and the occasional weekend biryani.
 We made friends in PGs, bonded over Maggi at midnight, and called home to ask which detergent to buy.
That tiny room held our ambitions, insecurities, and the first drafts of who we were becoming.
It wasn’t perfect, but it was ours.

The first day felt big — and we felt small.
 No WiFi, no curtains, just a ceiling fan that made too much noise and a future that felt too far.

We cried quietly that first night, missing home.
 But we woke up, dressed up, and showed up — because we had dreams.
 Because our parents had belief.
 Because this was the beginning.

We learned to eat alone.
 – To ask strangers for directions.
 – To stretch salaries till the last rupee.
 – To survive heartbreaks and skipped meals.
 – To stand on our own feet — one uncertain step at a time.

That room saw our anxiety, our growth, our becoming.

So, if you’re in that chapter now —
 Hold on. You’re doing better than you think.
 And if you’ve moved past it —
 Carry that version of you with pride.
 They got you here.

Did this resonate with you? What was your “starting-out” story? Share it below — someone out there will relate. 👇

App design inspired by TRP dads. We’ve peaked as a country.
Because what else do you call an app UI that looks like it’s about to scream "The nation wants to know!"
I opened it expecting dinner options.
Got served breaking headlines and bold fonts prime time chaos instead.
Zero warning. 100% dad-core.
✅

🔊 No overexplanation. If you get it, you get it.


🔊 Honestly louder than my actual father, and that’s saying something
Not a tribute. Not a campaign.
Just a perfectly chaotic shout-out to the man who thinks volume = engagement.
And I love that for us.

“𝐍𝐨 𝐣𝐨𝐛 𝐢𝐬 𝐬𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥. 𝐍𝐨 𝐡𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐬𝐡𝐚𝐦𝐞𝐟𝐮𝐥.”

Never be ashamed of the work that puts food on your table.

In a world obsessed with titles and timelines, it's easy to feel like you're falling behind. But here's the truth:

👉 All honest work is good work.
Whether you're wearing a uniform, behind a counter, coding at midnight, or juggling shifts — your work matters. It deserves respect. You deserve respect.

If you're feeling stuck or unsure, remember:
🔹 Every job teaches something valuable.
🔹 There is dignity in every role.
🔹 There is pride in self-reliance.
🔹 There is strength in showing up every day, no matter how tough.
🔹 Small steps lead to big shifts.
🔹 Don’t compare your journey to someone else’s highlight reel.
🔹 Progress is personal, not a race.
🔹 The work that pays your bills is nothing to be ashamed of.
🔹 Success is about showing up consistently — not impressing occasionally.

Keep going.
Keep learning.
Keep showing up.

𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐭 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐠𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐝, 𝐧𝐨 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐡𝐨𝐰 𝐡𝐮𝐦𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐭 𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐤𝐬.
You're building something real — one day at a time. 🙌

json.content:
"𝐏𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐣𝐨𝐛𝐬. 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐭𝐨𝐱𝐢𝐜 𝐜𝐮𝐥𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬."

Salary isn’t the only reason people leave.

Yes, money matters—but so does meaning.

Pay gets them in. Culture keeps them.

💼 People resign when they feel:
– Undervalued
– Disrespected
– Ignored

Because beyond the paycheck, what employees truly want is:
– To be seen
– To be heard
– To be appreciated

A thriving workplace isn’t just built on compensation—
It’s built on culture, connection, and care.

Here’s what that looks like in action:
✅ Managers who listen, not just direct
✅ Celebrating small wins, not just big targets
✅ Mental health isn’t a taboo—it’s supported
✅ Flexibility is a given, not a reward
✅ Feedback is regular, honest, and two-way
✅ Inclusion isn’t a checkbox—it’s how teams thrive

𝐕𝐚𝐥𝐮𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐬𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐞𝐥𝐬𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬.

Because culture isn’t just what you say—it’s what your people feel every day.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 6 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

json.content:
"𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐚𝐮𝐭𝐨 𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐞. 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧. 𝐀 𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐮𝐥 𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫: 
𝐓𝐚𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐚𝐫—𝐢𝐭 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐧𝐞𝐞𝐝𝐬 𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐲."

An auto ride in Bengaluru turned into a lesson in strength, humility, and second chances.

Gayatri Gopakumar, an HR professional, shared a moving story:
Her auto driver, Surendra R, once worked in IT.

During the ride, he overheard her work call and gently asked,
“Sorry Ma’am, are you in HR? Could you help me get a job?”

That one question held a world of emotion—
✅ Years of experience
✅ A health setback
✅ And the courage to try again.

Here’s what I took away from this:

🔹 𝐍𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐉𝐮𝐝𝐠𝐞 𝐚 𝐏𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝐛𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐂𝐢𝐫𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞
 Surendra was once part of the IT industry—his uniform may have changed, but not his talent or character.

🔹 𝐊𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐁𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐋𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠
 A single conversation can reveal someone’s journey, pain, and strength. Listen with empathy—you might be someone's bridge back to hope.

🔹 𝐀𝐬𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐇𝐞𝐥𝐩 𝐢𝐬 𝐚 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐭𝐡, 𝐍𝐨𝐭 𝐚 𝐖𝐞𝐚𝐤𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬
 It takes courage to say, “Can you help me?” Especially after life has knocked you down.

🔹 𝐒𝐞𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐂𝐚𝐧 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞 𝐋𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐬
 Some people don't need handouts—they just need someone to believe in them again.

🔹 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐫 𝐁𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐤𝐬 𝐃𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐃𝐞𝐟𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐩𝐚𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲
 Illness, family responsibilities, or setbacks don’t erase years of experience. Skills can return, and so can confidence—with the right support.

🔹 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐇𝐑 𝐑𝐨𝐥𝐞 𝐈𝐬 𝐌𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐇𝐮𝐦𝐚𝐧 𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐧 𝐖𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞
 Sometimes, being in HR means seeing beyond resumes—into stories, grit, and potential.

To everyone out there navigating a restart:
Your journey matters.
Your experience counts.

🚆 𝟏𝟕 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐬. 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧. 𝐀 𝐇𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐜 𝐁𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐠𝐞.

Meet Professor Gali Madhvi Latha, Civil Engineering Professor at IISc Bangalore.

She, along with her dedicated team, spent 17 years making the Chenab River Railway Bridge a reality — the world’s highest railway bridge and an engineering marvel that stands tall as a symbol of India’s rising might.

🔶 What can we learn from her journey?

🔹 Patience is a superpower: Transformational work takes time. 17 years of persistence is proof.

🔹 Teamwork builds nations: Her collaboration with engineers, scientists, and policymakers shaped the future of infrastructure.

🔹 Vision > Visibility: She worked silently behind the scenes while creating something that will be seen for centuries.

🔹 Women in STEM can move mountains — and build bridges.

🔹 Engineering isn’t just science, it’s nation-building.

𝐋𝐞𝐭’𝐬 𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐛𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐰𝐡𝐨 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐤 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐩𝐨𝐭𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭 — 𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐛𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐝 𝐢𝐭 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐮𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐞.

json.content:
Someone sent me a picture of this Shaadi.com billboard and it instantly made me smile.

It’s such a clever way to grab attention, especially for people who have been on those classic monsoon drives around Mumbai where every turn seems to have a "Lover’s Point."

But what I really liked is how effortless and light this feels. No pressure. No drama. No over-the-top promises.

It speaks to young, independent people who are figuring out life, career, and relationships on their own terms.

It’s witty, super relatable (especially if you’ve ever done the whole Lonavala-Pune drive during rains), and unlike the usual serious or pushy ads we often see in this space.

Loved how simple and self-aware this campaign is.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 20 hours ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

json.content:
Metro rides are usually about catching up on texts or playlists. But today, the walls were speaking.

Literally.

One hoarding said:
“While you go through unread messages for 5 minutes, ₹4 crore is disbursed to beneficiaries through DBT.”
Another reminded me:
“While you enjoy 5 of your favourite songs in your playlist, 150 pucca houses are built under PM Awas Yojana.”

These weren’t statistics. These were perspective checks.

What stood out was not just the numbers, but how creatively and calmly they were framed.

No chest-thumping. Just quiet confidence in progress.
Sometimes, the most powerful storytelling is the one that uses your own timeline to show what’s happening behind the scenes.

#India2025 #PMAY #DirectBenefitTransfer #StorytellingInGovernance #11YearsOfModiGovernment

json.content:
𝐒𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐛𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐯𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐨𝐞𝐬 𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞𝐧’𝐭 𝐦𝐞𝐭 𝐦𝐲 𝐝𝐚𝐝! ❤️ 
Shikha Gupta (L.I.O.N) & Manan Gupta

This is my dad — the man who showed me what quiet strength looks like.

No lectures, no drama. Just leading by example — with grace, resilience, and the belief that no matter what life throws your way, you handle it with integrity (and a sense of humor).

Growing up, I didn’t realize it then — but every time he chose patience over anger, kindness over ego, and people over pride, he was teaching me leadership.

And today, those values guide me — as a professional, a parent, and a person.
Happy Father’s Day, Papa.

 Thank you for being my first teacher, my biggest cheerleader, and the softest place to land. ❤️ Dr. N. K. GUPTA

If your dad is just a call away—make it.
Call him, even if it’s just to say you love him.
Because for some of us, that call can never be made again.

If your father is still here — hold him a little tighter today.
Give him a hug if you can.
Because someone else is only left with memories today.

#FathersDay2025 #HappyFathersDay #Gratitude #Leadership #Fatherhood #LifeLessons #LeadershipBeginsAtHome #FatherDaughter  #Dad #Father #Papa #WorkAndFamily #RoleModel #LinkedInFamily #WhatInspiresMe

json.content:
𝐓𝐄@𝐑𝐒 𝐎𝐅 𝐏𝐑𝐈𝐃𝐄 💛 👮‍♂️ 𝐀 𝐬𝐚𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐬𝐚𝐢𝐝 𝐢𝐭 𝐚𝐥𝐥. 🙏

In a rare and emotional moment, N Venkateshwarlu, Senior Superintendent of Police and Deputy Director at the Telangana Police Academy, stood in uniform and saluted his daughter, N Uma Harathi, during her visit to the academy.

Uma, who secured AIR 3 in the UPSC Civil Services Exam 2022, is currently training as an IAS officer.

The image of a father—an accomplished officer himself—saluting his daughter wasn’t just symbolic. It was a moment that echoed far beyond uniform or title.

Here’s what this moment teaches us:
✅ Respect isn’t bound by age or rank — It’s earned through effort, resilience, and integrity.
✅ Legacy isn’t about following footsteps, but about forging your own path.
✅ True leadership lies in celebrating others’ success—even when it’s your child’s.
✅ Professionalism doesn’t dilute emotion—it honours it.

To every parent who quietly supports a dream, and to every child who rises on the back of that silent strength—this moment was for you.

𝐀 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐝 𝐟𝐚𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫. 𝐀 𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐝𝐚𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭𝐞𝐫.
𝐀𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐬𝐚𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐡𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐲 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫.

json.content:
💡 𝐀 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭, 𝐚 𝐧𝐨𝐭𝐞𝐛𝐨𝐨𝐤, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐦.

In the Philippines, a 9-year-old homeless boy was seen doing his homework under the glow of a McDonald’s light. His family had no electricity—but he had something even more powerful: unshakable determination.

A passerby took a photo of that moment. It went viral.

But more importantly, it changed his life.
Soon, that little boy—who once relied on streetlight and willpower—was offered a full scholarship. A chance at the education he had only dreamed of.

🌟 Lessons from a Streetlight Scholar 🌟
 🔹 𝐃𝐞𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐧𝐞𝐞𝐝𝐬 𝐧𝐨 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬
– Even in the harshest circumstances, the will to learn can shine through. Comfort is a privilege, but commitment is a choice.

 🔹 𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐬 𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐲
– One photo. One share. One moment seen by the world turned into a scholarship. When we amplify the right stories, change follows.

 🔹 𝐓𝐚𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐢𝐬 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐚𝐥. 𝐎𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐢𝐬 𝐧𝐨𝐭.
– Potential exists everywhere—from classrooms to street corners. What makes the difference is access.

 🔹 𝐇𝐨𝐩𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐢𝐨𝐮𝐬
– The boy’s story sparked support, generosity, and global encouragement. When people see raw perseverance, they respond with kindness.

 🔹 𝐒𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐬 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐬𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐛𝐢𝐠 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞
– A stranger took a photo. That one act became the bridge between darkness and a brighter future.

 🔹 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐫
– A single chance to study has the power to transform not just one child’s future, but an entire family's trajectory.

 🔹 𝐂𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐧 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐛𝐞𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐝 𝐛𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐟
– Never underestimate the strength, adaptability, and dreams of a child—especially when they have nothing but hope to hold onto.

This story isn’t just about poverty.
It’s about possibility.

🌟 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭. 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐦𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭. 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐚𝐜𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬. 🌟

𝐋𝐞𝐭’𝐬 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐮𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐜𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐬𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐰𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐧 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐯𝐞—𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐞. 🌍💡

𝐏𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫 𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 — 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐗𝐋𝐑𝐈 𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞.

If people are the heart of business, XLRI is where future HR leaders are born.

As someone exploring B-Schools, I’ve asked myself a big question:
“Where do I learn not just HR theory, but how to lead people with real impact — without pausing my career?”

And the answer kept pointing in one direction: XLRI’s Blended PGDM-HRM program.

🔹 𝐋𝐞𝐠𝐚𝐜𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐋𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐬
India’s oldest and most prestigious HR program — shaping the HR landscape for 70+ years.

🔹 𝐅𝐮𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞-𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐲 𝐂𝐮𝐫𝐫𝐢𝐜𝐮𝐥𝐮𝐦
From labour laws to HR analytics and leadership strategy — everything you need, nothing you don’t.

🔹 𝐅𝐚𝐜𝐮𝐥𝐭𝐲 𝐖𝐡𝐨 𝐖𝐚𝐥𝐤 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐓𝐚𝐥𝐤
Learn from top researchers, industry advisors, and thought leaders in people management.

🔹 𝐃𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐭, 𝐑𝐢𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬
Your peers come from across industries — IT, BFSI, manufacturing, healthcare, startups, and more.

🔹 𝐏𝐞𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐏𝐮𝐬𝐡 𝐘𝐨𝐮
A class filled with India’s brightest minds — challenging, competitive, and collaborative.

🔹 𝐀𝐥𝐮𝐦𝐧𝐢 𝐘𝐨𝐮’𝐥𝐥 𝐁𝐞 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐝 𝐓𝐨 𝐉𝐨𝐢𝐧
XLRI HR alumni are CHROs, global leaders, and change-makers — across industries and borders.

🔹 𝐀 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐖𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐘𝐨𝐮
Join an alumni network of XLRI HR professionals who are CHROs, global leaders, and people-first change-makers.

It’s a small, tight-knit cohort. Seats are limited — and every seat counts.
The focus is on depth, not just scale.

Choosing the Blended PGDM-HRM at XLRI Jamshedpur isn’t about placements — it’s about purpose.
It’s about sharpening your strategic edge, elevating your influence, and putting people at the center of business.

If you believe people strategy is the future of business strategy — 𝐗𝐋𝐑𝐈 𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐣𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐲 𝐛𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐬.

json.content:
“𝐅𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐚 𝐦𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐜’𝐬 𝐠𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐫𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐨𝐫𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫—𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐚’𝐬 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐈𝐀𝐒 𝐨𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐫.”

🔶 Ansar Shaikh became India’s youngest IAS officer at 21, clearing the UPSC exam on his first attempt.

🔶 Ansar Shaikh’s journey is a true example of how hard work and determination can change a life. He grew up in Shelgaon, a village in Maharashtra’s Jalna district. His family faced extreme poverty. His father was a rickshaw driver and struggled with alcohol. His mother worked on farms and suffered from domestic violence. Ansar’s brother had to quit school and work in a garage, while his sisters were married off early.

🔶 Despite all these problems, Ansar never gave up on his education. He scored 91% in his 12th standard and later graduated in Political Science from Fergusson College, Pune, with 73%. After college, he prepared for the UPSC exam for a year with coaching support.

🔶 In 2016, he cleared the UPSC Civil Services Exam on his first attempt, getting an All India Rank (AIR) of 361. Today, he works as an Additional District Magistrate (ADM) in West Bengal.

🔶 Lessons from India’s Youngest IAS Officer
🔹 𝐘𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐛𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐠𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐝𝐞𝐟𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐟𝐮𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞
→ Born into poverty, Ansar proved that circumstances can be beaten with perseverance.

🔹 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐦𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐮𝐥 𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐫
→ Despite financial struggles and social pressure, he pursued education with laser focus.

🔹 𝐅𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭-𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐬𝐮𝐜𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐧𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐲—𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐢𝐭'𝐬 𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐥𝐞
→ As the first in his family to graduate, he carried not just his dreams but his family’s too.

🔹 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐜𝐞𝐬
→ While others had privilege, he had grit—and that made all the difference.

🔹 𝐒𝐚𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐟𝐮𝐞𝐥 𝐚𝐦𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧
→ His brother left school to support his coaching. Success often comes from shared sacrifices.

🔹 𝐃𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐰𝐚𝐢𝐭 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐢𝐝𝐞𝐚𝐥 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬
→ He didn’t wait for life to get easier. He prepared amidst adversity and still cracked UPSC in his first attempt.

🔹 𝐅𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭-𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐬𝐮𝐜𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐞, 𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐥𝐞
→ With clarity, discipline, and focus, even India’s toughest exam can be conquered in one shot.

🔹 𝐈𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐞 𝐛𝐲 𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠, 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐛𝐲 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠
→ Instead of being bitter about his situation, he chose to build a better one.

json.content:
𝐇𝐞𝐫 𝐟𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐬𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐝𝐢𝐝𝐧’𝐭 𝐛𝐮𝐲 𝐥𝐮𝐱𝐮𝐫𝐲 — 𝐢𝐭 𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐭 𝐡𝐨𝐩𝐞.

Sneha Sharma, a newly appointed school teacher from Begusarai, Bihar, could have spent her first paycheck on herself.
Instead, she chose to invest in 120 underprivileged children from the slum area of Lohiya Nagar.

She bought them school bags, water bottles, and essential stationery — not as charity, but as a step toward dignity in learning.

These children often lacked even the most basic supplies to attend school. Sneha didn’t just notice — she acted.

In a world that often celebrates personal milestones, she turned hers into a collective victory.

Because true educators don’t just teach lessons — they live them.

🔶 Lessons from Sneha Sharma’s Selfless Gesture

🔹 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐬 𝐚 𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐢𝐜𝐞, 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐚 𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞
Despite limited resources, Sneha chose to give — showing that true generosity comes from the heart, not the wallet.

🔹 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐥 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐭 𝐛𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐭𝐡𝐲
Understanding the struggles of the children around her, she stepped up not just as a teacher, but as a mentor and changemaker.

🔹 𝐅𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, 𝐥𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐭
While many dream of spending their first salary on themselves, Sneha’s decision created a ripple effect of kindness and hope.

🔹 𝐒𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬. 𝐁𝐢𝐠 𝐝𝐢𝐟𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞.
A few school supplies may seem minor — but for those children, they meant dignity, motivation, and the tools to dream bigger.

🔹 𝐓𝐫𝐮𝐞 𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐞𝐱𝐭𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐬 𝐛𝐞𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐝 𝐭𝐞𝐱𝐭𝐛𝐨𝐨𝐤𝐬
By supporting children’s access to learning, Sneha exemplified what it means to educate — not just teach.

🔹 𝐊𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐬 𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬
Her act has already touched hearts across the country — reminding us that compassion is contagious.

𝐀 𝐬𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐚𝐜𝐭. 𝐀 𝐛𝐢𝐠 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐜𝐭.
𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐭𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐫. 𝟏𝟐𝟎 𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐦𝐬 𝐬𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐞𝐝. 𝐂𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐭𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐡𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐝.

They say strategy solves everything.
But even the best of us forget the most basic things sometimes.

Like the other day, juggling between client calls, partnership decks, and campaign plans, I completely forgot my own meeting that I had set up. 😅
I realised it while sitting in traffic, mentally sorting through my to-do list (a strategist’s brain never sleeps).

And right then, this Vadilal hoarding caught my eye:
"Sab moh maya hai! Zara muskuraiye, Vadilal khaiye."

I actually laughed out loud. The universe, in sync with a billboard.
Sometimes, you don’t need a strategy. You just need to pause and smile. 🥹

What started as a Twitter thread tracking VC Platform roles has turned into 100 newsletters, 3.1K+ subscribers, 4K+ jobs posted, countless kind words and job placements, and a goldmine of data insights. 

I genuinely believe VC Platform is one of the most exciting, creative, and underrated roles in venture, and I wanted to shine a light on it – to make it more visible, more accessible, and less mysterious. 

To celebrate 100 issues, I pulled together the biggest trends from the last ~2 years of job drops: what’s changed, what’s stayed the same, and where we’re headed.

If you've subscribed, submitted a role, shared the newsletter, did an interview, or sent encouragement along the way...thank you. 💙

Here’s to the next 💯 !

I first met Cherae Robinson when we got her to speak at digitalundivided's first virtual conference. She is one of a very small handful of Black women who have raised >$1M (last I checked in 2022, there were only 98). 

We reconnected when we both pivoted into VC Platform roles and it's been a joy and an honor to have her in my corner. I've learned so much from how she approaches her work with such genuine intention, thinking through every single detail, while also being a master operator, automating the ish out of everything to give her back time. 

She's seen it and experienced it from both sides of the table. If you're a first-time founder, I cannot recommend this opportunity more. Even if you have to fly out for it – you'll be one of the lucky ones who also get to have Cherae in their corner.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 2 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
"How did you get into VC?" a college friend asked me recently.

My answer revealed something important: most of us in venture take wildly different paths to get here, but once we arrive, we often start looking for founders who fit a narrow mold.

My journey was unconventional—science nerd → NGO idealist → tech founder building in Africa → COVID biz casualty → VC. I'm also a DJ, foodie, and always know where to catch a vibe.

As a first-time founder, I raised money and built a real business, but I didn't get the kind of exit investors (or frankly, I) dream of. I watched my life's work get valued at "zero" on a spreadsheet and it SUCKED. That experience shined a glaring light on the disadvantage of not knowing what you don't know—even with amazing investors, I still faced critical blind spots from hiring to go to market to models to shutdown decisions, and the list goes on.

Here's what I learned: First-time founders can be AMAZING and still lack the context, relationships, and deep know-how that serial entrepreneurs take for granted—and it not only makes the journey harder, it can impact outcomes. These missing pieces of the puzzle can create an invisible ceiling.

I'm excited to put my path and passions to work and do something about this - including putting my money where my mouth is with a $50K investment.

Introducing: The First-Time Founder Food Club.

Once a month, we'll break bread, swap stories, and demystify the journey.

What you can expect:

• Incredible food (I have a 200+ restaurant database called "NY In My Belly")
• Well-curated, intimate gatherings (max 8 founders)
• Real talk with people who get what it's like to do this for the first time
• Access to hand-picked, experienced founders, operators, and investors who want to see you win
• Direct investment opportunity (I'm investing $50K via Next Wave NYC in at least one company from the club)

Apply to join here: https://lnkd.in/erxrpmy7

As Aubrie Pagano said at the 100 Women in AI Celebration: "Your lived experience is your competitive advantage."

Time to make that actually true.

Read the full post, see the application criteria, and apply here: https://lnkd.in/ehcfiWTB

PS: If you want to partner on this, hit me up. I'm down to collab if the vision is shared and the vibes are immaculate.

#FirstTimeFounders #AI #Venture #Community #NYC

json.content:
Copy & paste these prompts to improve your LinkedIn profile.

These 10 prompts will turn your LinkedIn profile Job-Ready: ⤵️


1. Write a job-focused LinkedIn headline:

“Create a LinkedIn headline for someone looking for a [Enter your target job] that clearly shows skills and the type of role they want.”


2. Write a LinkedIn summary to attract recruiters:

“Write a LinkedIn summary for a job seeker in [Enter your field] that shows their experience, skills, and what kind of job they are looking for.”


3. Create a professional experience section:

“Write a clear and results-focused experience section for a [Enter your job title], highlighting achievements and relevant job duties.”


4. List top skills for your LinkedIn profile:

“Suggest top 10 skills for someone in [Enter your field or role] to include on LinkedIn that match common job postings.”


5. Write a job-seeking status update:

“Write a LinkedIn post for someone actively looking for a job in [Enter your industry], sharing what kind of roles they’re open to and their key strengths.”


6. Write an ‘Open to Work’ section:

“Write a short and clear ‘Open to Work’ message for a LinkedIn profile to help recruiters understand the kind of roles you want.”


7. Highlight your top achievement:

“Write a short LinkedIn post where a [Enter your profession] shares a major achievement relevant to the kind of jobs they want.”


8. Write a strong LinkedIn intro message to recruiters:

“Create a message a [Enter your job title] can send to recruiters on LinkedIn to show interest in job openings and introduce themselves.”


9. Optimize your education section:

“Write the education section for a [Enter your role or field] on LinkedIn, focusing on relevant courses, honors, and certifications.”


10. Add certifications and courses to LinkedIn:

“List 5 valuable certifications or online courses someone in [Enter your field] should add to their LinkedIn profile to look more job-ready.”

postedAt.postedAgoText: 23 hours ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

json.content:
10 ChatGPT Prompts To Learn Anything 10x Faster:

[ Save for later ]


1. Mind Mapping

🧠 "Guide me in creating a comprehensive mind map for [insert topic]. Include key concepts, subtopics, and connections to enhance my visualization and understanding of the material."


2. Feynman Technique

🧠 "Help me explain [insert topic] in simple terms as if teaching it to a young child. This should ensure I grasp the fundamental concepts clearly."


3. Metaphorical Learning

🧠 "Illustrate the concept of [insert topic] through metaphors or storytelling. Use relatable examples to make the learning experience engaging and memorable."


4. Spaced Repetition Schedule

🧠 "Create a spaced repetition learning schedule for [insert topic]. Include specific intervals for review sessions and brief quizzes to reinforce memory retention over time."


5. Growth Mindset Development

🧠 "Provide insights on how to cultivate a growth mindset in relation to [insert topic]. Focus on strategies that encourage resilience, persistence, and continuous improvement."


6. Achieving Flow State

🧠 "Guide me on how to achieve a state of 'flow' while learning [insert topic]. Include tips for maintaining focus, minimizing distractions, and enhancing my overall learning experience."


7. Curiosity-Driven Exploration

🧠 "Generate a list of intriguing questions or exploration tasks related to [insert topic] that will stimulate my curiosity and encourage independent
learning."


8. Scenario-Based Learning

🧠 "Create a series of hypothetical scenarios where I can apply knowledge related to [insert topic]. This should help me develop problem-solving skills and practical understanding."


9. Feedback Mechanism

🧠 "Design a feedback loop for assessing my understanding of [insert topic]. Include methods for self-assessment and ways to track my progress over time."


10. Reverse Engineering Concepts

🧠 "Assist me in reverse engineering [insert topic]. Break down complex ideas into simpler components to facilitate better understanding and application."

json.content:
Struggling to find great talent?

AI finds them before your competitors do.

12 Impactful ChatGPT prompts to find the best talents: ⤵️


1. Write a job description that attracts top talent:

“Create a clear and engaging job description for a [Enter job title] role that highlights key responsibilities, required skills, and company benefits to appeal to top candidates.”


2. Craft a candidate outreach message:

“Write a friendly and professional LinkedIn message to reach out to a potential candidate for a [Enter job title] role, encouraging them to apply.”


3. Improve a job post for better visibility:

“Enhance this job posting for a [Enter job title] role to make it more engaging and keyword-optimized for better visibility on job boards.”


4. Write screening questions for interviews:

“Generate a list of screening questions to evaluate candidates applying for a [Enter job title] position based on required skills and experience.”


5. Draft a candidate rejection email:

“Write a polite and respectful rejection email to inform a candidate that they have not been selected for the [Enter job title] position.”


6. Prepare behavioral interview questions:

“List behavioral interview questions to assess soft skills like teamwork, communication, and problem-solving in candidates for a [Enter job title] role.”


7. Create a follow-up email after an interview:

“Write a follow-up email to send to a candidate after their interview for a [Enter job title] role, sharing next steps in the hiring process.”


8. Generate offer letter content:

“Draft the content for an offer letter to a candidate selected for the [Enter job title] position, including job details, compensation, and start date.”


9. Write a candidate evaluation summary:

“Summarize the strengths and weaknesses of a candidate interviewed for a [Enter job title] role to help the hiring team make a decision.”


10. Design a recruiting email campaign:

“Create a series of email messages for a talent outreach campaign targeting experienced [Enter profession] professionals.”


11. Build a talent pipeline strategy:

“Outline a simple strategy for building and maintaining a talent pipeline for [Enter industry or department].”


12. Write a job ad for social media:

“Create a short and catchy job ad for a [Enter job title] opening to post on LinkedIn, Instagram, or Twitter.”

30 Job Search Engines...

[ Save Now ]

• FlexJobs - Curated remote and flexible job listings.

• We Work Remotely - Large remote work community with various job listings.

• Remote.co - Remole job board and remote work resources.

• AngelList - Startups hiring for remote positions.

• Hubstaff Talent - Free resource of remote freelancers and agencies.

• Jobspresso - Hand-picked remote jobs in tech, marketing, and more.

• Remote OK - Remote job listings across multiple categories.

• Working Nomads - Curated remote jobs for digital nomads.

• Remotive - Remote jobs in tech and startup sectors.

• SkipTheDrive - Remote job listings in various industries.

• Virtual Vocations - Remote job listings with detailed company profiles.

• Pangian - Global community and remote job board.

• Outsourcely - Remote jobs for startups and established companies.

• Dynamite Jobs - Curated remote jobs in various fields.

• Remote4me - Remote job listings categorized by job type.

• Remote Jobs Club - Newsletter featuring remote jobs.

• Crossover - Remote jobs with competitive salaries.

• CloudPeeps - Freelance and remote job opportunities.

• JustRemote - Remote job listings from around the web.

• EuropeRemotely - Remote jobs for Europeans.

• Remotees - Remote jobs from various companies.

• YunoJuno - Remote freelance jobs for creatives.

• Dribbble - Remote design jobs and freelance opportunities.

• The Muse - Remote jobs and career advice.

• Glassdoor - Company reviews and remote job listings.

I tested 137 productivity apps.

Most were trash.

These 8 actually improved my life.


1. Feedly

A dynamic RSS aggregator that brings together articles, blog posts, and updates from various platforms into a single, personalized interface.


2. Grok

xAI’s advanced conversational AI integrates real-time web search, reasoning abilities, and access to X’s data to provide the latest information.


3. Vozo

An AI-powered video platform that translates, rewrites, and redubs videos, utilizing voice cloning and lip-sync technology to create seamless multilingual content.


4. Fellow

This platform provides a collaborative meeting space where AI helps with note-taking, transcription, and managing meetings, including building agendas together.


5. Wrike

A complete work management solution that uses AI to help teams track projects, predict risks, and collaborate smoothly on tasks and workflows.


6. Tidio

A platform designed for customer service that includes live chat, AI-powered chatbots, and help desk features to manage customer questions and help online businesses increase their sales.


7. Timehero

A clever project management tool that automatically plans your tasks and balances the workload according to your team’s availability and priorities.


8. HeightApp

A smart project management tool that takes care of everyday tasks and backlog updates automatically, while letting your team collaborate live using spreadsheets, Kanban boards, and Gantt charts.

json.content:
AI is flooding the job search space right now.

Resume generators. Chrome extensions. Auto-apply bots. LinkedIn outreach tools.

Everyone’s saying: “We’ll get you hired faster!”

But here’s my checklist for what actually matters:
Does it help one save time without destroying quality?

Blasting 500 low-quality apps doesn’t help if none of them are a fit. 

Does it continuously learn and improve?

If there’s no feedback loop (like “what got a reply”), it’s just guessing.

Is it always transparent about what's written on those applications?

Some tools fill apps behind the scenes. You don’t even know what’s submitted. 

That’s risky.

Does it still feel human?

The best tools amplify you. They don’t replace you.

I’m bullish on this space, but only for the right kind of AI.

There are tools built just to automate.

And tools built to augment.

We’re building for the second group. Because this isn’t about shortcuts.

It’s about leverage.

Don't miss early access. We’re only taking 500 → bloomhq.ai

article.linkLabel: Open article: Bloom - AI-Powered Job Finder Platform | Automate Job Search by bloomhq.ai

json.content:
A Job Search Routine That Works
One of the biggest mistakes I see?

People are job hunting in random bursts of panic.

Here’s a calm, sustainable routine that works:

Monday – Research & Shortlist 🗓 
Pick 15–20 high-quality roles. Prioritize new ones (<7 days old).

Tuesday – Resume Tailoring 🛠 
Use AI to tailor your resume for 3–5 top roles. Focus on the top third.

Wednesday – Outreach 📬
Send 3–5 short DMs to hiring managers. Be specific, not spammy.

Thursday – Apply & Track🚀 
Apply to your shortlist. Log everything (Bloom helps here, too).

Friday – Reflect & Iterate🔁 
Did you get replies? If not, try changing the subject line or your resume headline.

Consistency beats chaos.

And this system works.

Your smarter job search starts here. Join the waitlist → bloomhq.ai

We didn’t set out to build a company.

We were just trying to help a few friends find internships.

But the deeper we got into their process, the more frustrated we became.

Endless tabs.
Rewriting resumes.
Filling the same forms over and over.

And still… no responses.

We watched one friend spend 4 hours on a single application.

She never heard back.
That stuck with us.

It wasn’t a lack of effort. It was a broken process.

We kept wondering:
"Why isn't there a tool that just does this for you?"

So we started building one.

It’s called Bloom. A job search AI that finds roles, fills out forms, and applies for you.

We’re not trying to disrupt anything.
We’re just building what we wish existed when we were students.

Job boards are broken. Bloom's our fix.

Early access (500 spots) 👉 https://bloomhq.ai/

json.content:
Job Search Like a Marketer

Most people apply to jobs like they’re doing homework:
“Follow instructions. Submit. Hope.”

But what if you approached it like a marketing campaign?

Here’s the 4-step system I share with Bloom users:

Segment your audience
Not every company needs the same approach. 

Break them into:
Dream companies
Growth-stage startups

“Safe bets” where you meet 90 %+ of the criteria

Write your offer
Your resume isn’t your product. You are.
What’s your pitch? What pain do you solve?

Run outbound
Don’t just wait for job boards. DM hiring managers. Start convos.

Track & optimize
Use a CRM (or Bloom!) to track status and follow-ups.

This isn’t about spamming 200 roles.
It’s about being strategic.

Job search = funnel. Treat it like one.

Launching soon. First 500 users get early access → bloomhq.ai

🚨 450+ Job Seekers confirmed this

Since we started 🍄Bloom, we’ve talked to 450+ final-year students and grads across the U.S.

Some stuff didn’t surprise us:
Everyone’s using LinkedIn

Everyone’s applying to a lot of roles (50–200!)

Everyone’s overwhelmed

But a few things really hit: Job boards are a black hole

People were applying all day but had no idea where their time was going. 

Zero tracking. Zero replies.

ChatGPT was helpful… but not enough

Lots of folks used AI to improve their resumes. 

Few knew how to actually tailor them or beat ATS filters.

Outreach felt intimidating

Everyone wanted to message hiring managers. Most didn’t know what to say—or whether it’d even work.

So we asked:
What if job search felt more like using Notion? Or Spotify? Or Duolingo? Or even better - Tinder?

Easy to start

Smart nudges

Designed for you

Way faster than doing it all manually

We’re not fully there yet. But that’s what we’re building.

Because no one should be spending 20 hours a week sending resumes into the void.

Join our waitlist - https://www.bloomhq.ai/

json.Headline:
🚨 𝙅𝙐𝙎𝙏 𝙄𝙉: Coinbase secures EU 🇪🇺 Crypto License, and swaps Ireland for Luxembourg as main hub.

Coinbase has secured a license from Luxembourg to offer crypto services across the European Union and will make the country its central hub in the region.

The U.S. crypto exchange’s main European base has been in Ireland since 2023.

Coinbase said Friday that it obtained its Markets in Crypto Assets (MiCA) license from Luxembourg’s Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF).

MiCA is a sweeping regulation that aims to create a harmonized legal framework for crypto across all 27 EU member states. 

The rules, which came fully into force late last year, also aim to reduce risks for consumers buying crypto assets following a series of scandals in the sector.

It makes Coinbase the first U.S. crypto exchange to receive a MiCA license. 

Rival firm Gemini which is owned by the Winklevoss twins, is expected to receive its own EU license from Malta soon. Gemini chose Malta as its MiCA hub in January.

“Coinbase is all in on Europe, and we’re advocating for crypto’s future across the continent,” Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong told CNBC: https://lnkd.in/dJgeuAKb

json.Headline:
🚨 𝘽𝙍𝙀𝘼𝙆𝙄𝙉𝙂: Revolut is quietly creating its own stablecoin.

Here is what we know:

Revolut is continuing its pursuit of creating its own stablecoin, sources familiar with the matter told Decrypt:  https://lnkd.in/d85dP_HB

The development comes amid a growing list of non-crypto companies now considering issuing their own stablecoins as the regulatory environment shifts in the United States and around the world.

The London-based bank launched Revolut X, a centralized crypto exchange available across the European Union, in 2024: https://lnkd.in/dhwS3825

The company is now eyeing its own stablecoin to further its crypto venture, two sources familiar with the company's plans told Decrypt. 

Rumors of a Revolut stablecoin have been circulating for months (https://lnkd.in/dh5mT9Tz), though the neobank is now in conversation with at least one crypto-native company regarding the venture, one of the sources said.

json.Name: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/roan-dollmann-a34234171_fintech-payments-ecommerce-activity-7340366303691526146-0q8d
json.Headline:
The 1990s: When Online Payments Were Born

Part 1 of 4 – The Road to Payment Orchestration

Payment orchestration is one of the hottest topics in payments today and for good reason.

It’s what allows modern businesses to route transactions through multiple PSPs, reduce failed payments, optimize for cost and performance, and stay agile across markets. Think of it like air traffic control for your payment stack, making sure every transaction gets where it needs to go, smoothly and efficiently.

But orchestration didn’t appear overnight.

It’s the result of decades of evolution in how we move money online.

So, over the next four posts, I’m tracing that evolution:
The 1990s, 2000s, 2010s, and 2020s.
Each era brought a major shift, and each one built the foundation for where we are now.

Let’s start at the very beginning.

The 1990s: When Online Payments Were Born

Amazon and eBay were launching.
The internet was still a curiosity to most.
And online payments? A wild experiment.

There were no payment platforms as we know them today.
No Stripe. No PayPal. No smart routing.

Just early gateways like CyberCash (1994) and Authorize.Net (1996),  primitive tools that allowed merchants to accept card payments online... if they were lucky.

These systems were crude:
🔹Manual reconciliation in spreadsheets
🔹Almost zero fraud controls
🔹Only basic US card support
🔹No redundancy — one gateway per merchant

And yet, it worked
just barely.

The big question back then wasn’t “How do we improve checkout?”
It was: Can we even make online payments happen at all?

The answer was yes. But it came at a cost.
For every $1 inconvenience, merchants paid $10 in operational pain.
Still, the foundation was laid.

In Part 2, we’ll move into the 2000s
where developers demanded more flexibility and the API-first movement reshaped what was possible. 

#Fintech #Payments #Ecommerce #Paymentorchestration #Startups #Onlinepayments #Historyofpayments #RoanDollmann

json.Name: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/roan-dollmann-a34234171_paymenttokenization-securepayments-digitalpayments-activity-7340674645194407938-mmQ9
json.Headline:
What is Payment Tokenization?

Payment tokenization is the process of replacing sensitive payment data (like your credit card number) with a unique, non-sensitive placeholder, which is a token.

The original data is stored securely in a vault.
The token is what gets used in the transaction, and it’s worthless if intercepted.

Example:
When you add your card to Apple Pay, your actual card number is never stored on the device or shared with the merchant.

Instead, it’s replaced with a token, which is a randomly generated code that acts as a stand-in.

That token is useless on its own.
If stolen, it’s just a string of numbers with no connection to your card.
The real card data? Safely stored in a secure vault.

Why does this matter?

Tokenization helps:
🔹Prevent card data theft
🔹Reduce PCI compliance burden
🔹Enable one-click checkouts and recurring billing
🔹️Power mobile wallets and invisible payments
🔹Safely route transactions across multiple providers

Without tokenization, every transaction is a security risk.
With it, you get speed, safety, and scale.

It’s not a luxury.
It’s the foundation.

#PaymentTokenization #SecurePayments #DigitalPayments #Tokenization #CardSecurity #PCICompliance #InvisiblePayments #PaymentProcessing #FintechExplained #OneClickCheckout #MobileWallets #PaymentInfrastructure #FintechEducation #DataSecurity #RoanDollmann

json.Name: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/roan-dollmann-a34234171_fintech-payments-paymentorchestration-activity-7340725009063043072-t2x1
json.Headline:
The 2000s: When Payments Went Programmable
Part 2 of 4 – The Road to Payment Orchestration

The 1990s proved that online payments could work.
The 2000s asked: how can we make them better?

Developers were done settling for clunky, rigid gateways.
They wanted control, flexibility, and most of all, simplicity.

Enter the API-first movement.

For the first time, developers could integrate payments directly into their apps with code. No faxing forms. No 6-week onboarding. No cryptic legacy software.

The shift was massive.

In the 2000s, we saw:
 🔹 The rise of PayPal, making online payments consumer-friendly
 🔹 The launch of Braintree (2007), with cleaner APIs and tokenization
 🔹 The early days of mobile and app commerce
 🔹 A growing global appetite for digital transactions

And critically
we moved from gateways to platforms.

Platforms that abstracted the mess of acquiring banks, card schemes, and compliance headaches while giving developers more power than ever before.

But there was still a catch...

Merchants had to choose one provider and live with the trade-offs:

Want better global coverage? Too bad, your PSP isn’t strong in LATAM.
Payment failures rising? You’re stuck until they fix it.
Negotiating fees? No leverage when you’re locked in.

The orchestration era hadn’t arrived yet.
But the groundwork was being laid.

Up next: The 2010s — where Stripe, Adyen, and others took payments mainstream and merchants began demanding real control.

#fintech #payments #paymentorchestration #startups #onlinepayments #developerstack #APIfirst #RoanDollmann

json.Name: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/roan-dollmann-a34234171_fintech-payments-paymentorchestration-activity-7341115775417577473-wV2Q
json.Headline:
The 2010s: Platforms, Power, and the Push for Control
Part 3 of 4 – The Road to Payment Orchestration

By the 2010s, payments had gone mainstream.

Stripe launched in 2011.
Adyen went public in 2018.
Square was already powering millions of small businesses.

Payments were no longer a problem to solve, they were a strategic growth lever.
APIs were clean, fast, and well-documented. Developers loved it.

But merchants? They started seeing the limits.

Suddenly, they weren’t just looking for a gateway or a processor.
They wanted:
🔹Global scale
🔹Smart routing
🔹Better approval rates
🔹Multiple PSPs — without multiple integrations

This era gave rise to... fragmentation.

Merchants stitched together their own stacks:
Stripe in the US, Adyen in Europe, local acquirers in Brazil, PayPal on the side.
It worked but it was fragile.

There was no single layer orchestrating it all.
No intelligent routing, no fallback logic, no unified reporting.

Payments were powerful, but messy.

This complexity is what gave rise to the next evolution:
Orchestration as a purpose-built layer.

Next: The 2020s — where orchestration became a must-have, not a nice-to-have.

#Fintech #Payments #Paymentorchestration #Stripe #Adyen #Square #Psps #Onlinepayments #RoanDollmann

json.Name: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/roan-dollmann-a34234171_6amld-aml-fintechcompliance-activity-7340311247118036993-Fc_p
json.Headline:
6AMLD: The Anti-Money Laundering Rule That Could Impact Your Fintech - Even If You're Not in the EU

When Revolut froze thousands of accounts...
When Binance got fined over €4 billion...
When N26 and Wise tightened onboarding...

It wasn’t just about KYC. It was about liability.
Enter the 6th Anti-Money Laundering Directive (6AMLD)
Europe’s quiet revolution in compliance law.

Here’s what it means:
➡️ “Enabling” financial crime is now a crime.
If your platform facilitates money laundering even unknowingly, you’re accountable.
➡️ Corporate liability is official.
It’s not just compliance officers anymore. Under 6AMLD, companies can be criminally charged.
➡️ Those fuzzy gray areas? They’re now black and white.
➡️ Minimum 4-year sentence. EU-wide.
 No more country-by-country leniency. Harmonization = high stakes.

Why fintech should care:
 🔹 BaaS platforms are now risk carriers
 🔹 PSPs onboarding sub-merchants at scale
 🔹 Cross-border apps managing high volumes 

If you're part of the value chain - you're part of the compliance chain.

This isn’t just EU regulation.
This is a global signal: Regulators expect prevention, not just reporting.
Startups can no longer afford to treat AML as a checkbox.

I'm curious to hear how others are adapting: what’s your approach to liability in the age of embedded finance?

#6AMLD #AML #FintechCompliance #Revolut #Binance #N26 #Wise #EmbeddedFinance #BaaS #RegTech #FinancialCrime #EURegulation #StartupCompliance #RoanDollmann

Fintech events are like payments, the best ones just work. Money20/20 Europe is no exception. 

Here’s what we got up to last week in Amsterdam. 

🎤 On stage: Emilie Mathieu, Rory O'Neill, Meron Colbeci, and Milan Jani joined a strong line-up of industry voices to discuss everything from trust to identity to embedded finance. Thanks to the speakers, moderators, and everyone who made the sessions sharp, focused, and full of perspective.

🏃 On the move: Our morning 5K run with Fintech Run Club helped start the day off right, setting the pace and making space for deeper connections beyond the conference floor. Thanks to XXL Nutrition for the goodie bags that kept energy levels up.

🍴 Around the table: Private dinners hosted meaningful discussions and shared perspectives that will shape what’s next in fintech.

📍 On the ground: Our team showed up ready to listen, learn, and connect.

Take a look through the photos to see what we’ve got up to on stage, around the table and on the move.

json.content:
What if your customers didn’t browse your store, but their agent did? 🤖 

Forget endless scrolling, agentic commerce is about AI doing the shopping for you. It finds the right item, confirms it matches the brief and pays for you. 

In our latest blog post Checkout.com, explains what agentic commerce is, why it matters, and how to prepare for the future of shopping. 

Jorn Lambert, Chief Product Officer at Mastercard and Jack Forestell, Chief Product and Strategy Officer at Visa also shared their thoughts with us.

“The future of commerce will be powered by agentic AI, and it’s arriving faster than we could have ever imagined. Mastercard’s longstanding partnerships across the payments ecosystem, including our work with Checkout.com, are critical to delivering secure, seamless payments into the heart of AI-powered experiences,” said Jorn Lambert, Chief Product Officer at Mastercard. “We designed Mastercard Agent Pay to empower AI agents to transact safely and responsibly, grounded by the same trust and control consumers expect from Mastercard. Our work with Checkout.com is helping to directly enable businesses to meet customers where they are – inside the conversation.”

"We're big believers that AI agents will transform how people around the world shop and pay, and the first step is to ensure that payments are safe, secure and in the consumer’s control," said Jack Forestell, Chief Product and Strategy Officer at Visa. "With Visa Intelligent Commerce, we’re connecting the tech, payments and AI ecosystems to build the first-generation of trusted AI Commerce and we’re excited to partner with innovative companies like Checkout.com to bring this vision to life.”

Is your business ready for this next era? 👉  Find out 
 https://lnkd.in/edyK5sDZ

Much of  Money20/20 Europe is inevitably about regulation, tech, infrastructure and some healthy guess work on AI (!)...so it was great to join a panel to discuss how marketing can help fintech brands differentiate in the long term.

We unpacked how fintech CMOs are staying close to business performance and gaining currency to build brands for the long term. 

Here were my key takeaways:
📈 It's obvious, but as a commercial function marketing should speak the lingua franca of the business.  Anything that is measured that is not is sheer vanity

🤝 Marketing has a responsibility to share all work with other commercial teams and move in sync. These partnerships build currency.   

📊 In a world of razor thin product differentiation, it's imperative to pick one thing your audience cares about and keep talking about it to build relative differentiation.  

🤖 AI has removed many of the irritable tasks for marketers, but we have to go back and redesign our work to liberate disruptive moves. 

Fintech operates within two driving forces that are in tension - regulation and innovation - what matters most is the creation of trust in order to simplify peoples world.

Session was covered well by Payment Expert https://lnkd.in/e5BnK35s

Thanks to Soha Hohnecker (she/her), Yewande Akomolafe-Kalu, and Kathryn Frankson, to Money20/20 Europe for the invitation, and to the audience for starting the day early!

#Money2020EU #WhereTheWorldChecksOut

json.content:
Ever wonder what actually happens when you click "Pay Now"?

Online payments may feel instant, but behind the scenes, there’s a complex web of handshakes, approvals, and verifications.

Here’s what happens—step by step:

1. Checkout
You enter your card or choose a payment method (like PayPal, Klarna, or Apple Pay).
→ This data is captured and encrypted.
 
2. Payment Gateway
Think Stripe, Adyen, or PayFirmly.
→ The gateway securely routes the transaction data to the payment processor.
 
3. Payment Processor
The processor (like FIS, Worldpay, or Checkout.com) contacts:
🔹Card networks (Visa, Mastercard, etc.)
🔹Your issuing bank (the bank that gave you the card)
 
4. Authorization
Your bank checks:
 🔹Is the card real?
 🔹Is there enough balance?
 🔹Is the transaction suspicious?
If approved → a temporary hold is placed. If declined → you’re shown an error.
 
5. Response Sent Back
The approval or decline flows back through the processor → gateway → merchant website.
→ You see “Payment Successful.”
 
6. Settlement
At the end of the day, funds are moved between banks via batch clearing.
→ This is when the money actually starts moving.

Bonus: Orchestration
Platforms like PayFirmly offer smart routing, fallback logic, and local optimization across 500+ methods, so your payment doesn’t fail just because one processor is down.

In short:
What looks like one click is actually a high-speed relay race between 5+ players.
 
Next time your payment fails… it’s probably not your fault.

#Fintech #Payments #eCommerce #DigitalPayments #PaymentProcessing #PayFirmly #HowItWorks #Stripe #Adyen #Visa #PayPal #Klarna #Mastercard #SmartRouting

json.content:
Technological breakthrough in the US 😊

A funny news item came across yesterday. A couple of days ago in the subway in Washington, DC appeared payment by bank card. Previously, there was no such option in the subway of the American capital. This “innovation” was met with great applause.

In #Moscow, the possibility of paying for a fare with a bank card appeared back in 2015.

And for the past couple of years in Russia in stores, cafes, restaurants, etc. you can pay with just a smile. This is when you approach the terminal, just smile - and the payment is made.

This video was made by an American blogger in Moscow, which many people didn't believe - and accused of being a fake 😊


P.S. “Pay with a smile” is a service of "Sber" (Sberbank) , which allows you to pay for purchases at checkout counters in stores using biometrics. The project was launched in the summer of 2023 as an alternative to Google Pay and Apple Pay payment services that have left the market. Initially, “Smile Pay” could only be used by customers of Sberbank, but from the end of 2024 the service became available to customers of any credit organizations.

#RussianLanguageDay

Сегодня, 6 июня, мы отмечаем Международный день русского языка — праздник, учреждённый ООН в 2010 г. в честь дня рождения великого русского поэта Александра Сергеевича Пушкина.

Пушкин — это не только основоположник современного русского литературного языка, но и культурный мост между эпохами и народами. 

Его язык — живая ткань русской души, а его стихи цитируют даже те, кто не говорит по-русски.

🖋️ "Я памятник себе воздвиг нерукотворный..." — эта строка известна далеко за пределами России, как символ силы слова и вечной ценности культуры.

В мире, полном перемен, язык остаётся опорой идентичности и важнейшим инструментом диалога.

📚 Сегодня — отличный повод вспомнить, что язык формирует мышление, объединяет людей и помогает строить мосты, а не стены.

С праздником всех, кто говорит, пишет, чувствует и думает на русском!

И любит русскую культуру.

#Russia #Pushkin #RussianLanguageDay

#RussiaToday

Dear friends, as you can imagine, I couldn't miss today's Forum of the Future 2050 in #Moscow, where the headliner was the rock star of world diplomacy, #SergeyLavrov :)

At the panel discussion "#MultipolarWorld in the 21st Century" Lavrov "lit it up").

"Now, too, we are talking about the fight between good and evil." The Russian minister noted that the #West and #Europe are on the side of evil.

"And not all of Europe, by the way. But this aggressive core, led by Starmers, Mertzes, Macrons," Lavrov emphasized.

"They are fighting against us, supplying Ukraine with high-precision, high-tech weapons that Ukrainians cannot control, which means that the citizens of those countries that supply these weapons control them."

"In addition to this, they also want to demonstrate the isolation of our country by prohibiting everyone from coming here," Lavrov noted.

Participants of the discussion:

Moderator – #DimitrySimes, American-Russian historian and publisher of the American magazine The National Interest

#ErrolMusk, engineer, businessman, gem trader, father of #ElonMusk

#GeorgeGalloway, master of British politics

#LarryJohnson, former CIA and US State Department analyst

#KarinKneissl, former Foreign Minister of Austria

#ZhangWeiwei, is a Chinese professor of international relations at Fudan University and the director of its China Institute.

Lavrov: "Multipolarity is a geopolitical reality."

On the #UN: "It would be best to move the UN to #Sochi" - thunderous applause and laughter in the audience 😊

On #Merz and #Germany: when Merz says that Russia will not stop at Ukraine, he judges by himself. This is genetic revanchism. We are not fighting for territories, but for the Russian people.

About the UN: "What did the UN do to anyone?" Everything is written correctly there, it is only necessary to implement it - and implement it not selectively, as the West prefers, but systematically.

Sergey Lavrov recalled the mass murder of Russians in #Odessa.

"To the #shame of the entire #West - uninvestigated crimes, including such significant ones as the burning of fifty people alive in the House of Trade Unions in Odessa on May 2, 2014."

And also about other crimes, which the collective West chose not to pay attention to and spoke out about the hypocrisy of the so-called collective West, which itself provoked the tragedy in #Serbia, tearing away Kosovo, and then ... pretended that the events in Ukraine in 2014 do not imply the right of people to self-determination.

More precisely, only those who are under the patronage of Western curators can self-determination.

Well, I will finish the story with another symptomatic quote from Sergey Lavrov: “Right now, Russia’s best diplomats are fighters on the front lines.”

P.S. Thank you, #KonstantinMalofeev (Head of #Tsargrad), for organizing this cool event!

#Russia #FairPlay #Dialogue

json.content:
#LavrovToday
Europe once again rallying under Nazi flag against Russia – #Lavrov

The top diplomat has hit out at Ukraine’s #EU backers over their renewed push to inflict a strategic defeat on #Moscow.

The EU has failed to learn the lessons of history by once again gathering under a Nazi flag to attempt to inflict a strategic defeat on Russia, Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Tuesday.

Lavrov was responding to remarks by German Chancellor #FriedrichMerz, who on Monday declared there were “no more range limitations” for weapons sent to Ukraine by the West, including Germany. 

#Merz has also vowed to turn the German military into Europe’s strongest conventional force.

“#Europe has once again found itself a Nazi flag by committing to a completely misguided, disastrous venture of inflicting a strategic defeat on Russia,”- #SergeyLavrov stated.

“To hear from the current German leader that #Germany will regain its position as the leading military power in Europe, just after we have celebrated the 80th anniversary of the defeat of Hitler’s Nazism, is quite symptomatic. History, apparently, taught these people nothing.”

Merz’s remarks on long-range weapons drew concern even within his own coalition. German Vice Chancellor and Finance Minister #LarsKlingbeil told reporters shortly after the chancellor’s statement that no agreements had been reached regarding the range of Ukrainian strikes using German weapons on Russian territory beyond what the previous government had in place. 

Lavrov said Merz’s confused and uninformed position “reflects the level of competence of modern European leaders.”

While Germany has not supplied long-range weapons to #Ukraine, Merz’s comments have stirred speculation about a possible policy shift, particularly regarding the delivery of Taurus cruise missiles. Former Chancellor Olaf Scholz consistently rejected the idea, warning it would dangerously escalate the conflict.

Lavrov also addressed remarks by French Foreign Minister #JeanNoelBarrot, who on Monday claimed #France is not at war with Russia.

“He’s not telling the truth. France is fighting against Russia because its long-range missiles are being used by the Nazi regime in Kiev to attack the territory of the Russian Federation. And these are mainly attacks on civilian targets,” Lavrov said.

He stressed that the only path to ending the Ukraine conflict lies in halting Europe’s militaristic course.

“The main thing here... is to ensure that Europe stops sabotaging the movement towards peace, which is supported by both the #UnitedStates and #Türkiye, and to which the #RussianFederation is fully committed,” Lavrov said.
 
#RussiaToday #Russia #Putin #FairPlay #CommonSense

#RussiaToday

#VladimirPutin: Russia stands for an equal and indivisible security architecture for all countries, but "not at the expense of the security and interests of other countries."

Attempts to isolate #Russia have failed and have shown how outdated the previous unipolar world order is.

Now the emphasis is on mutual respect for the interests of all countries. This was stated by Vladimir Putin in a video address to the participants of the XIII International Meeting of High Representatives in Charge of Security Issues. It is being held in #Moscow.

Vladimir Putin

"We are convinced that the new security architecture must be equal and indivisible, that is, all states must receive firm guarantees of their own security, but not at the expense of the security and interests of other countries.

The experience of uniting states in the fight against evil, against Nazism, against militarism, the understanding of the colossal price that humanity paid for peace and freedom, for the right of peoples to choose their own future path of development, made it possible to lay the foundations of the post-war world order, to create the #UN — a universal, legitimate organization based on the principles of international law, thanks to which many geopolitical trials were overcome.

Today, it is especially important to protect the truth about the events of those years, to fight against attempts to rewrite history, to question the decisive contribution of the peoples of the Soviet Union to the victory over Hitler's Germany, to glorify Nazi criminals and their accomplices.

Quite recently, on May 9, we solemnly celebrated the anniversary of Victory in the Great Patriotic War.

The celebration in Moscow became another symbol of unification around the ideals of the Great Victory, once again demonstrating the determination of our friends and partners to form a safer world, to constructive cooperation and joint solution of global problems." 

Delegations from more than 100 countries are participating in the meeting, including representatives of the US Embassy. The Ukrainian conflict is among the main topics of discussion.

Russian Foreign Minister #SergeyLavrov in his speech drew special attention to the double standards of the West.

Sergey Lavrov

"It is very sad that the #West reacts only to our legal, fair, response measures to the terrorist actions of the Kyiv regime, which, I emphasize, affect civilian targets, while our response measures concern exclusively strikes on military targets and facilities used by the Ukrainian armed forces.

We will ensure that everyone understands the essence of what is happening, and that yet another attempt to shield the #Kiev regime, now for its direct involvement in terrorism, is exposed."

#Russia #Putin #Lavrov #Multipolarity #FairPlay

json.content:
#PutinToday

20,000 people from 140 countries are taking part in the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum. This year's theme is "Common Values ​​- the Basis for Growth in a #MultipolarWorld "

#VladimirPutin
"We need a fundamentally new development model, built not on the rules of #neocolonialism, when the so-called golden billion pumps out the resources of other countries in the interests of a narrow circle of so-called elites," Putin said.

Global challenges certainly require a global response. It is impossible to solve problems alone or at someone else's expense. Only joint actions can ensure the movement of the entire #civilization forward.

The #BRICS countries set the bar in the development of nuclear energy, the IT industry, and the AI ​​sphere. The principles of the organization are parity and openness to new participants. Russia invites partners to contribute to new global growth.

The global economy is undergoing the largest transformation in recent decades.

If at the beginning of the 21st century the BRICS countries made up a fifth of the world economy, now this figure has grown to 47 percent. It is obvious that this figure will grow.

"This is, as they say, a medical fact. This will happen inevitably."

"As for the payment infrastructure, here we will continue to closely cooperate with our BRICS colleagues, build effective, reliable and, I repeat, independent of external interference mechanisms and services," Putin said.


About the state of the #RussianEconomy

• "The Russian economy must become more technological. And this is not a wish, this is an objective requirement of today and tomorrow. A challenge that must be met if we want to become stronger. And we do."

• According to Putin, the idea of ​​the Russian economy as a raw materials economy is "outdated." "The contribution of the raw materials component to the economic dynamics of our country is no longer decisive. Our economy is not just developing confidently, but is becoming more qualitative, complex and multifaceted."

• "Despite the difficult external environment, Russia's GDP has increased by more than 4% annually over the past two years, i.e. it has grown at a rate higher than the #globalaverage," the president said. In particular, non-oil and gas GDP growth in 2023 was 7.2%, and in 2024 — 4.9% (RBC)

• Russia has achieved a record reduction in poverty: at the end of last year, it was 7.2%. In 2000, the poverty rate in Russia was 29%.

• Unemployment is 2.3%, a historical minimum.

• Youth unemployment in Russia is 7.5%, one of the best rates in the #world.

• "Overall, the level of wages in Russia is still, of course, insufficient." Russia needs to move to an economy of high wages based on increased labor productivity.

• The most important task for 2025 is the transition of the Russian economy to balanced growth.

#Russia #Putin #Dialogue #FairPlay #Multipolarity

🚨 Coinbase launches Stablecoin Payments service for E-Commerce.

Here is what you should know:

Coinbase is launching a platform designed to make stablecoins a go-to payment method for online transactions, a potentially big leap forward in the mainstream adoption of cryptocurrencies meant to track the US dollar. 

The announcement follows the passage of landmark stablecoin legislation in the US Senate on Tuesday (see video below👇) and comes as large retailers have expressed interest in exploring stablecoins for use as payments in online transactions, a roughly $𝟲 𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗱𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆. 

Such use of this type of cryptocurrency threatens to circumvent card networks Visa Inc. and Mastercard Inc. and other online payment services.

“We built the new system to mimic credit-card rails so it slots into existing flows with zero disruption,” a Coinbase spokesperson said in a statement: https://lnkd.in/ddb2ZuDz

Coinbase Payments is targeting online platforms like Shopify Inc.and eBay Inc., a prized client segment among payment processors since the websites provide distribution to thousands of small-to-medium sized businesses who are often looking for ways to avoid the fees associated with accepting card payments.

The Coinbase payments products include a checkout suite to help consumers easily pay from a crypto wallet provided by Coinbase Wallet, MetaMask or Phantom, among others. 

Stripe recently announced plans to acquire Privy, which helps merchants embed crypto wallets into their websites:  https://lnkd.in/dYUdbisA

That allows them to accept stablecoin payments without requiring customers to exit to a third-party site to set up a wallet, which adds friction to the buying experience and lowers the chance of a customer completing their purchase.

Source video: CNBC

json.content:
Nubank is now the 𝗽𝗿𝗶𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗯𝗮𝗻𝗸 for 𝟮𝟭.𝟳% of Brazilians 🤯

Probably nothing... 

A recent study by consultancy Okiar reveals that Nubank has become the main bank for 21.7% of Brazilians 🇧🇷 in 2025, surpassing traditional institutions like Itaú and Bradesco.

In the first half of 2025, Okiar surveyed over 1,000 Brazilians to analyze their relationships with financial institutions. 

The research showed that, on average, each person holds four bank accounts, highlighting the wide range of options in Brazil’s banking sector.

According to Okiar, the top six primary banks in Brazil are:

1️⃣ Nubank – 21.7%
2️⃣ Itaú – 14.2%
3️⃣ Bradesco – 12%
4️⃣ Caixa Econômica – 10%
5️⃣ Banco do Brasil – 9.9%
6️⃣ Santander – 8.1%

While Nubank ranks first as the most frequently cited “main bank,” it sits in third place when ranked by total number of customers. 

Caixa Econômica leads in overall customer base, followed by Bradesco, with Itaú and Banco do Brasil rounding out the top five.

This gap highlights a shift in consumer behavior, while traditional banks still hold the largest customer bases, digital banks like Nubank are increasingly becoming users’ primary financial institutions.

The study also found that customers of traditional banks use an average of ten different financial products, compared to eight for digital bank customers. 

This suggests that, despite the rise of digital banking, traditional banks continue to offer a broader range of services and maintain a strong presence in specific market segments.

Source/more info: https://lnkd.in/d9NpZSZi

json.content:
🚨 𝙅𝙐𝙎𝙏 𝙄𝙉: Coinbase secures EU 🇪🇺 Crypto License, and swaps Ireland for Luxembourg as main hub.

Coinbase has secured a license from Luxembourg to offer crypto services across the European Union and will make the country its central hub in the region.

The U.S. crypto exchange’s main European base has been in Ireland since 2023.

Coinbase said Friday that it obtained its Markets in Crypto Assets (MiCA) license from Luxembourg’s Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF).

MiCA is a sweeping regulation that aims to create a harmonized legal framework for crypto across all 27 EU member states. 

The rules, which came fully into force late last year, also aim to reduce risks for consumers buying crypto assets following a series of scandals in the sector.

It makes Coinbase the first U.S. crypto exchange to receive a MiCA license. 

Rival firm Gemini which is owned by the Winklevoss twins, is expected to receive its own EU license from Malta soon. Gemini chose Malta as its MiCA hub in January.

“Coinbase is all in on Europe, and we’re advocating for crypto’s future across the continent,” Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong told CNBC: https://lnkd.in/dJgeuAKb

json.content:
The cold email system that finally worked 👇🏻
 
In 2024, I struggled with cold emails.
 
I sent hundreds of emails, and guess what?
 
No replies.
 
Spam filters blocked most of them.
 
The ones that got through? Completely ignored.
 
I realized the problem wasn't just spam filters, it was that my entire approach was broken.
 
Here's how I turned things around:
 
1️⃣ Fixed my deliverability first
 
I stopped blasting emails blindly and started warming up my email accounts properly. With Saleshandy, I could connect unlimited email accounts and rotate between senders automatically. Their sequence score feature showed me exactly what was wrong with my emails before I even hit send.
 
2️⃣ Built campaigns that scale
 
Using Saleshandy's automated sequences, I created multi-step campaigns with personalized follow-ups. The sub-sequences feature let me move interested prospects into different flows automatically based on their replies. No more manual sorting.
 
3️⃣ Tracked everything that mattered
 
Saleshandy's unified inbox brought all replies from multiple accounts into one place. Their AI categorized responses automatically like positive, not interested, out of office. I could finally see what was working and double down on it.
 
The results?
 
- Open rates jumped from 15% to 45%
- Reply rates went from zero to 21%
- Closed 160+ leads for my clients
 
I could manage everything from one dashboard. No more juggling between tools or paying per inbox like other platforms charge.
 
Saleshandy changed how I think about cold outreach. It's not about sending more emails. It's about sending the right emails to the right people at the right time.
 
Over to you: What's your biggest challenge with cold emails right now?
